title: typeof and Array.isArray Check
description: Predict typeof quirks and correct array detection.

questions.explanation: `typeof null` is the famous historical quirk: it returns `"object"`. Test null with `value === null`.
Arrays are objects in JavaScript, so `typeof []` is `"object"`. Use `Array.isArray` to detect arrays.

questions.prompt: Which expression correctly detects an array?
questions.explanation: `Array.isArray` is the reliable built-in. `typeof` has no `"array"` result, and length checks false-positive plain objects.
Only array values return true. `null` is not an array.

questions.prompt: What should `classify(null)` return in a type-guard function?
questions.explanation: Because `typeof null` is `"object"`, classify null with `value === null` first and return a dedicated `"null"` label.
